title: IREN Energizes First Phase of 2GW Sweetwater Data Center Campus
text: IREN announced the successful energization of its 1.4GW Sweetwater 1 data center site in Texas. The 2GW Sweetwater Hub is strategically located in West Texas across two sites: Sweetwater 1 and Sweetwater 2, spanning 2,200 acres. Energization marked the connection of the site's high-voltage substation to the ERCOT grid, representing a key milestone in the development of the broader 2GW Sweetwater campus. Power delivery at Sweetwater 1 is expected to ramp progressively in line with the phased construction and commissioning of data centers at the site. The two Sweetwater sites are located outside Sweetwater in Fisher County, West Texas and are set to total 2GW across 2,200 acres at full build-out. Energization on the 500-acre Sweetwater 2 is expected in 2028. The campus is being built as a 2.0 gigawatt compute platform designed to host next-generation artificial intelligence infrastructure at industrial scale, functioning as a long-term growth platform designed from the ground up to host hyperscale artificial intelligence clusters. The campus is capable of supporting over 700,000 state-of-the-art liquid-cooled GPUs.
